reason: The 3.9.0 discussions docs say sync tasks run through `asgiref.sync.sync_to_async` in a thread and that the GIL prevents CPU parallelism, and worker code runs sync tasks via `utils.sync_to_async(thread_sensitive=False)`.

The sync-defer guide says sync calls on an opened async app only work inside `sync_to_async` and otherwise raise `RuntimeError`; that path uses `AsyncToSync`, whose installed source contains the exact quoted error message.

The docs explicitly state that with a synchronous connector the only supported action is deferring tasks, and worker-related async connector methods raise `SyncConnectorConfigurationError` in 3.9.0 source.
`SQLAlchemyPsycopg2Connector.open(engine=...)` reuses the provided SQLAlchemy engine/pool, and when no engine is passed it creates one internally via `_create_engine(...)`.

The official worker FastAPI example uses lifespan startup with `run_worker_async(install_signal_handlers=False)`, then on shutdown calls `worker.cancel()` and awaits `asyncio.wait_for(worker, timeout=10)`.

`run_worker_async` documents `queues=None` as listen-to-all; missing imported task definitions are documented to fail with `TaskNotFound`, and worker source shows that task-not-found failures do not produce retry decisions (so they are not retried).
The production schema docs show `kwargs: {"options": "-c search_path=myschema"}` and explicitly note this can let multiple Procrastinate instances share one PostgreSQL database.
